title: Charania: Jaylen Brown ‘never requested a trade’ from Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown reportedly never requested a trade from the Boston Celtics before being traded to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Celtics initially offered Jaylen Brown in a package for Giannis Antetokounmpo, but later traded him for Paul George and draft picks.
Despite trade rumors, Brown expressed a desire to remain in Boston and maintain a strong relationship with the Celtics.

title: 2 men involved in 2015 Dorchester home invasion, murder sentenced after conviction, guilty plea
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Victor Arrington sentenced to life without parole for 2015 Dorchester home invasion and murder.
James Boyd receives five years probation after guilty plea; has already served 10 years in prison.
Wrong-home invasion resulted in the murder of Richard Long; fiancée survived and testified.

title: What to know about heat warnings, cooling centers in Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston is under an excessive heat warning through Friday, with temperatures expected to reach a heat index of 112 degrees.
City officials activated cooling centers, pools, and libraries to provide relief and encourage residents to stay hydrated.
Free museum admissions are available during the heat wave, including the Museum of Science and the Institute of Contemporary Art.

title: Dartmouth Mall rolls out new limits on youth visiting mall without an adult
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dartmouth Mall requires under-18s to be with a 21+ adult on weekends after 5 p.m., effective July 3.
Policy aims to ensure safety, applies across the mall, excluding stores with separate entrances.
Minors working at the mall can still work unsupervised but must show proof of employment and go directly to work.

title: Bruins reportedly acquire defenseman Will Borgen in trade with Rangers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins trade for Rangers defenseman Will Borgen, giving up a 2027 second-round pick and a conditional 2028 third-round pick.
Borgen, a 29-year-old right-shot blueliner, fills a top-four role, replacing Brandon Carlo who was traded in 2025.
Borgen signed for four years at a $4.1 million cap hit, appeared in 75 games last season with five goals and 10 assists.

title: Celtics reportedly send Jaylen Brown to 76ers in stunning trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown traded by the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks.
The trade includes Paul George, a 2028 first-round, a 2031 unprotected first-round, and two second-round picks.
Brown, a 10-year Celtics veteran and 2024 NBA Finals MVP, joins a notable Philadelphia 76ers roster.

title: Sewage overflow issue in Merrimack River is resolved, officials say, although some beaches are still closed
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sewage overflow into the Merrimack River from a wastewater main break in Haverhill has stopped, officials announced.
The overflow discharged 8 million gallons of sewage daily, prompting swimming closures at beaches downriver.
Officials advise avoiding contact with the river near Haverhill for 48 hours due to potential bacteria presence.

title: Judge in Karen Read wrongful death suit probes leak of Proctor’s ‘sensitive’ information
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judge Mark Gildea investigates a potential leak of sensitive medical information in Karen Read's wrongful death lawsuit.
Gildea considers suspending Michael Proctor's discovery after impounded details appear on social media shortly after submission.
Gildea emphasizes the need for confidentiality and integrity, warning attorneys against using social media to influence the case.

title: These are the beaches closed in Mass. as the heat wave hits
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Two sewer main breaks in Haverhill spilled millions of gallons of untreated wastewater into the Merrimack River.
Local and state officials closed several Massachusetts beaches due to increased bacteria levels from the spill.
The state's beach water quality dashboard updates hourly to inform on current beach closure statuses.

title: 17 books to read for America’s 250th anniversary
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston.com and local booksellers compiled a list of 17 books for America's 250th anniversary.
The reading list includes histories of America's founding and critiques of its modern challenges.
Books recommended come from three independent bookstores and Boston.com staff selections.
